basic_details.job_title: PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 3289 Assistant Lineman Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL), PSEB Head Office, The Mall, Patiala-147001 (Punjab)
basic_details.short_information: Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL) has published Advertisement No. CRA 317/26 dated 07 June 2026 for recruitment of 3289 posts of Assistant Lineman (ALM). PSPCL, a Power Generating and Distribution company owned by Government of Punjab, is looking for young and dynamic candidates for the post of Assistant Lineman. Candidates holding Matriculation and National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in Lineman Trade along with Punjabi language knowledge of at least Matriculation level are eligible to apply online at pspcl.in. The application process is active from 15 June 2026 to 06 July 2

important_dates.date of online test (tentative): To be notified on PSPCL website (www.pspcl.in)
important_dates.admit_card_date: At least 7 days before the exam date
important_dates.document checking: To be notified later on pspcl.in and through registered email

application_fee.details: Fee will be accepted by online mode only. No other mode for depositing fee is acceptable. The fee once paid shall neither be refunded under any circumstances nor held in reserve for any other recruitment or selection. Submission of application shall be considered complete only if the application fee has been deposited and showing "Paid" in the online application form. GST @ 18% is applicable as per Government of India norms.

age_limit.details: As per Punjab Govt. Notification G.S.R.20/Const./Art.309/Amd.(10)/2010 dated 24.05.2010, candidates should not be below 18 years and above 37 years of age as on 01/01/2026. Age relaxation in upper age limit is admissible as per PSPCL/Punjab Government instructions.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions up to the last date of submission/registration of online application i.e. 06-07-2026. The candidate must possess the requisite qualifications up to the last date and shall submit proof at the time of document checking. Candidates whose result has been declared recently and certificate is yet to be issued — the date of declaration of result shall be considered as the date of completion of that qualification, subject to production of proof. | Educational Qualification

vacancy_details.Name of Category: PWD (PD) - Person with Disability (Partially deaf/hearing, Autism Spectrum Disorder (mild), Specific Learning Disability, Mental illness)

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 19,900/- (Basic Pay) per month as per 7th CPC/Pay Matrix and PSPCL financial circular no. 21/2022 dated 17.08.22

selection_process: Part Sr. No. Topic No. of Questions Marks
Part-I (Qualifying Test of Punjabi Language) 1 Knowledge of Punjabi Language 50 Questions (MCQ) 50 Marks
Part-II 1 Questions related to concerned discipline of the post applied (Technical Questions - Lineman Trade) 50 Questions 50 Marks
2 Punjabi Language Knowledge 20 Questions 20 Marks
3 General Knowledge 10 Questions 10 Marks
4 Reasoning 10 Questions 10 Marks
5 Arithmetic 10 Questions 10 Marks
Total (Part-II) 100 Questions (MCQ) 100 Marks
Exam Duration 3 hours (180 minutes)
Question Type Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ) - 1 mark for each correct answer
Negative Marking No negative marking
Language of Part-II Bilingual (English/Punjabi) except Punjabi language questions which will be in Punjabi only. Candidates can choose language (English/Punjabi) for Part-II during exam.

Part-I Qualifying Marks Minimum 50% marks (i.e. 25 marks out of 50) in Punjabi Language (Part-I) is mandatory. Failure to secure minimum qualifying marks in Part-I will disqualify the candidate irrespective of marks in Part-II.
Part-II Minimum Qualifying Marks General Category: 25% | Reserved Category (EWS, SC, BC, Ex-Serviceman, Freedom Fighter, Sports Person, PwD): 20%
Normalization In case of examination held in multiple shifts, normalization of marks will be applied to neutralize the effect of difference in difficulty level of each set of papers.
Tie-Breaking If two or more candidates secure same marks in Part-II, their relative merit shall be determined by age — higher age candidate shall be placed at higher merit.
Exam Centre Any Centre in Punjab/Chandigarh (to be intimated through admit card on PSPCL website). No change of test centre is permissible.

Merit Preparation Final merit list is prepared based on marks secured in Part-II of CBT only (except Sports Person category whose merit is prepared by O/o Director Sports, Punjab as per Sports gradation policy).

The selection process for PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026 consists of a Computer Based Online Test followed by Document Checking. The online test is based on the Punjab Civil Services (General and Common Conditions of Service) Rule
2022 notification dated 28.10.2022. No TA/DA will be paid for journeys performed for online test/document checking.
Computer Based Online Test - Exam Pattern
Key Exam Rules:
how_to_apply: Candidates will apply online through the PSPCL website www.pspcl.in in English only. No other means/mode of submission including manual/paper applications will be accepted. The application process is in three steps: Step-I (Registration and Form Filling), Step-II (Online Fee Payment), and Step-III (Print Application Form). Candidate should have a valid personal mobile number and personal e-mail ID which should be kept active during the entire recruitment process.

Candidates are advised not to wait till the last date and should submit their application and deposit the fee at the same time. PSPCL shall not be held responsible if any candidate is unable to submit application and fee due to last-minute rush or any internet/bank transaction issue.
Recent passport size coloured photograph in JPG/JPEG format (Dimensions: 150 x 200 pixels preferred; Size: 20 KB to 50 KB)
Signature on white paper with Black ink pen in JPG/JPEG format (Dimensions: 140 x 60 pixels preferred; Size: 10 KB to 20 KB)
Documents to be Produced at the Time of Document Checking (Originals + One Self-Attested Photocopy):
Matriculation or equivalent certificate and marksheet (for basic qualification and age proof)
National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in Lineman Trade
Punjabi language pass certificate of at least Matriculation level
Punjab Domicile proof (for claiming reservation benefits)
SC/BC Certificate as per instructions of concerned department of Govt. of Punjab (if applicable)
EWS Income and Asset Certificate showing gross annual income for FY 2025-26 valid for FY 2026-27 (if applicable)
Ex-servicemen Certificate/Lineal Descendant Certificate issued by District Sainik Welfare Officer, Govt. of Punjab (if applicable)
Freedom Fighter Certificate issued by Deputy Commissioner of concerned district (if applicable)

Sports Gradation Certificate issued by Director Sports Department, Punjab (if applicable; must be submitted to Recruitment Section, Head Office, PSPCL, Patiala before or up to the last date of online application)
PwD Certificate issued by Civil Surgeon of Govt. of Punjab for partially deaf/hearing, Autism Spectrum Disorder (mild), Specific Learning Disability, Mental illness (if applicable)
Any other document as required by the document checking committee
Apply Online at: https://www.pspcl.in → Recruitment tab → Recruitment for the post of Assistant Lineman against CRA 317/26
important_instructions: Online applications only - no other mode will be accepted; application without deposit of requisite fee shall be rejected. Category/sub-category once filled in the online application cannot be changed under any circumstances; candidature of a candidate who applies under wrong category will be rejected without further notice. On successful registration, candidates are advised not to attempt re-registration for the same post; only the latest application (with highest/latest application number) shall be entertained and fees paid against one application number shall not be adjusted against any other. For EWS category, it is mandatory to produce Income and Asset Certificate showing gross annual income for FY 2025-26 valid for FY 2026-27; candidates who have not yet obtained this certificate are advised to apply immediately to concerned authorities. Sports Person category candidates must submit their Sports Gradation certificates to the Recruitment Section, Head Office, PSPCL, Patiala before or up to the last date of online application submission. Only certificates issued by O/o Director Sports, Punjab are valid. Candidature of a candidate is purely provisional subject to fulfillment of eligibility after document checking; mere issue of admit card/passing of online examination will not imply candidature has been finally cleared. Candidates appearing for document checking must appear personally; nobody on the candidate's behalf can appear. Two chances (the second being final) will be given for document checking; failure on both occasions will result in cancellation of candidature. Selected candidates issued appointment letter will be given 21 days to join PSPCL; failure to join within the stipulated period will result in automatic cancellation of the appointment letter. Candidates working in Govt./Quasi Govt. offices/PSUs must submit a No Objection Certificate from their employer at the time of joining. Validity of selection panels will be one year from the date of approval of 1st selection panel by the competent authority; waiting list shall cease after expiry of panel validity. Mobile phones, cell phones, hand bags, electronics/non-electronics instruments, ornaments etc. are strictly not allowed in the examination complex. At the exam venue, candidates must bring Admit Card and Aadhaar Card along with one more photo identity proof in original (Employer ID (Govt.), Driving License, Voter Card, Passport). For any queries/issues in applying online, contact: [email protected] quoting the registration number. In case of any ambiguity/dispute, decision of PSPCL shall be final and binding. Legal jurisdiction shall be subject to Local Courts at Patiala only. PSPCL may depute selected candidates for on-the-job training as per prevailing PSPCL rules and regulations.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for PSPCL Assistant Lineman Recruitment 2026 (CRA 317/26) is 06 July 2026. The last date for deposit of fee is 10 July 2026. Candidates are strongly advised not to wait till the last date and should submit the application and deposit the fee simultaneously. The application window opens on 15 June 2026 at pspcl.in.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 3289 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant Lineman (ALM) in PSPCL under Advertisement No. CRA 317/26. These include 1456 backlog posts and 1833 new posts across various categories such as General (715), SC MZB (544), SC OT (541), BC (440), PWD (347), XSM (129), EWS (183), Sports Person (132), and others. PSPCL reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of posts at any stage.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for PSPCL ALM 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Matriculation or equivalent examination from a recognized Board. Additionally, they must possess a National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in Lineman Trade. Candidates must also have passed Punjabi language at least at Matriculation level or equivalent. All qualifications must be obtained up to the last date of submission of online application i.e. 06 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For General, EWS, BC, and other candidates (except SC and PwD), the application fee is Rs. 1200/- + 18% GST = Rs. 1416/- plus bank charges if applicable. For Scheduled Caste and Person with Disability category candidates, the fee is Rs. 750/- + 18% GST = Rs. 885/- plus bank charges if applicable. The fee is non-refundable and must be paid online only through SBI Payment Gateway via Internet Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card.

faq.question: Q5. What is the exam pattern for PSPCL ALM 2026 online test?
faq.answer: The Computer Based Online Test consists of two parts: Part-I is a qualifying Punjabi Language test of 50 MCQ questions (50 marks) where minimum 50% (25 marks) is mandatory; failure disqualifies the candidate irrespective of Part-II marks. Part-II has 100 MCQ questions (100 marks) covering Technical Questions related to Lineman trade (50), Punjabi Language (20), General Knowledge (10), Reasoning (10), and Arithmetic (10). Total duration is 3 hours with no negative marking.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 37 years of age as on 01/01/2026. Age relaxation is provided to SC/BC candidates (5 years), Physically Handicapped/PwD candidates (10 years), Ex-servicemen of Punjab domicile (as per Armed Forces service deduction), and Widows/certain categories of women (upper age limit up to 40 years). Serving employees of PSPCL/PSTCL/Punjab Govt. get relaxation to the extent of their service rendered.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the PSPCL ALM 2026 online test?
faq.answer: No, there is no negative marking in the PSPCL ALM 2026 Computer Based Online Test. All multiple-choice questions carry one mark for each correct answer. However, candidates must note that Part-I (Punjabi Language) is a mandatory qualifying test requiring minimum 50% marks, and the final merit is prepared only on the basis of marks secured in Part-II of the online exam.

faq.question: Q8. How will the final merit list be prepared for PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The category-wise final merit list will be prepared based on marks secured in Part-II of the Computer Based Test only (for all categories except Sports Person). For Sports Person category, the final merit will be prepared by O/o Director Sports, Punjab as per the Sports gradation policy. If the exam is held in multiple shifts, normalization of marks will be applied. In case of a tie, the candidate with higher age will be placed at higher merit.

faq.question: Q9. What are the minimum qualifying marks in Part-II of PSPCL ALM 2026 exam?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifying marks in Part-II of the online test are 25% for General Category candidates and 20% for Reserved Category candidates (EWS, SC, BC, Ex-Serviceman, Freedom Fighter, Sports Person, and PwD). Candidates who fail to secure these minimum marks in Part-II will not be considered in the final merit list even if they have qualified Part-I (Punjabi Language test).

faq.question: Q10. What documents are required at the time of PSPCL ALM document checking?
faq.answer: Candidates must produce originals along with one self-attested photocopy of: Matriculation certificate (for basic qualification and age proof), NAC in Lineman Trade, Punjabi language pass certificate (Matriculation level), Punjab Domicile proof, and relevant reservation certificates (SC/BC/EWS/XSM/FF/SP/PwD as applicable). EWS candidates must produce Income and Asset Certificate for FY 2025-26 valid for FY 2026-27. Sports Person category candidates must have already submitted their Sports Gradation Certificates to the Recruitment Section, PSPCL, Patiala before the last date of application.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for PSPCL ALM Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for PSPCL Assistant Lineman Recruitment 2026 (CRA 317/26) is 06 July 2026. The last date for deposit of fee is 10 July 2026. Candidates are strongly advised not to wait till the last date and should submit the application and deposit the fee simultaneously. The application window opens on 15 June 2026 at pspcl.in.
